ir_specs.statement: Print the number of processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Print the number of processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.

ir_specs.statement: If this information is not accessible, then print the number of processors installed.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If this information is not accessible, then print the number of processors installed.

ir_specs.statement: If the OMP_NUM_THREADS or OMP_THREAD_LIMIT environment variables are set, then they will determine the minimum and maximum returned value respectively.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If the OMP_NUM_THREADS or OMP_THREAD_LIMIT environment variables are set, then they will determine the minimum and maximum returned value respectively.

ir_specs.statement: Linux cgroup version 2 CPU quotas may also limit the maximum returned value.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Linux cgroup version 2 CPU quotas may also limit the maximum returned value.

ir_specs.statement: The result is guaranteed to be greater than zero.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The result is guaranteed to be greater than zero.

question_specs.question: Print the number of processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.
question_specs.explanation: Print the number of processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.

question_specs.question: Print the number at least from −20 (process has high priority and gets more resources, thus slowing down other processes) through 19 (process has lower priority and runs slowly itself, but has less impact on the speed of other running processes).
question_specs.explanation: Print the number of processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.

question_specs.question: If this information is not accessible, then print the number of processors installed.
question_specs.explanation: If this information is not accessible, then print the number of processors installed.

question_specs.question: If the OMP_NUM_THREADS or OMP_THREAD_LIMIT environment variables are set, then they will determine the minimum and maximum returned value respectively.
